title: Contract Tests, Fault Injection, Deployment, Upgrade, and Recovery · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first tested, recoverable RabbitMQ production release for contract tests, fault injection, deployment, upgrade, and recovery. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of tested, recoverable RabbitMQ production release while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers.
Inject return, nack, close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, and stale callback failures deterministically.
Roll out schema, topology, publisher, consumer, policy, and broker changes in a compatibility-safe order with shadow evidence and rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Layered Test Strategy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 3660 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate release on contracts, broker faults, compatible rollout, rollback, and supported upgrade path; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Layered Test Strategy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Layered Test Strategy, keep Layered Test Strategy connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.
